data.title: Maruti Suzuki profit misses estimates as cost surge, fall in other income squeezes margins
data.description:
Maruti Suzuki reported a surprise drop in quarterly profit on Tuesday, missing estimates, as rising costs and a sharp fall in other income offset gains from a tax cut-led recovery in vehicle demand in India.

Indian carmakers, which benefited from a recovery in demand after September tax cuts lifted showroom traffic and supported pricing in small cars and SUVs, are now facing margin pressure from rising costs despite record sales.

The Swift maker posted profit of 35.91 billion rupees ($380 million) for the quarter ended March 31, down from 38.57 billion rupees a year earlier and below analysts' estimates of 41.38 billion rupees, according to LSEG data. The company kicks off quarterly results for automakers in the country.

Maruti said raw material costs surged nearly 51%, lifting total expenses 28% from a year earlier and hurting profitability. Profit was also hit by a 67.3% drop in other income, which includes investment earnings.

Margins contracted 270 basis points to 7.2%.

Revenue at the Brezza SUV maker rose to 524.49 billion rupees, helped by higher volumes and improved realizations.

The company, majority-owned by Suzuki Motor, was among the biggest beneficiaries of the tax cuts, posting a 3.7% rise in domestic sales.

Maruti said sales were constrained by production limits, with about 190,000 pending customer orders at year-end, including nearly 130,000 for small cars, while dealer inventory stood at a lean 12 days.

Small-car sales fell 4.3% in the quarter but were up 2.3% for the full financial year.

Overall sales, including exports and supplies to Toyota under their global manufacturing and design partnership, rose nearly 11.8% from a year earlier.

($1 = 94.5087 Indian rupees)

data.title: JetBlue's quarterly loss widens as high fuel costs dent margin recovery
data.description:
JetBlue Airways reported a bigger first-quarter loss on Tuesday, as surging jet fuel costs driven by the Iran war weighed on margins, threatening to derail the carrier's turnaround efforts.

The U.S.-Israeli attack on Iran has closed the critical Strait of Hormuz, disrupting nearly a fifth of global oil and gas supplies and triggering the aviation industry's biggest shock since the COVID-19 pandemic.

"While the macro environment, particularly fuel, has become more volatile, we are taking decisive actions to manage what is within our control, including adjusting capacity, optimizing revenue, and maintaining disciplined cost control," CEO Joanna Geraghty said on Tuesday.

The airline plans to recoup 30% to 40% of the increased fuel costs in the second quarter, with expectations to recover all of it by early 2027.

Jet fuel prices have nearly doubled since the conflict erupted at the end of February, squeezing carriers between spiraling costs and tickets already sold at prices they cannot adjust.

U.S. carriers face added pressure after abandoning fuel-cost hedging. Fuel is the second-largest airline expense after labor, typically accounting for a fifth to a quarter of operating costs.

Rising fuel costs heighten the pressure on smaller carriers like JetBlue, which have limited financial flexibility and greater exposure to uncertainty. Industry executives warn the Middle East conflict could reshape airline industry dynamics.

JetBlue entered 2026 aiming to regain stability and start reaping the benefits of a turnaround effort launched in 2024, focusing on tight cost controls, route optimization and deferring aircraft deliveries. But high fuel costs threaten to derail that plan.

CEO Joanna Geraghty told employees last week that JetBlue was not considering bankruptcy, according to a memo seen by Reuters, adding the carrier had ample liquidity and access to additional capital.

The New York-based carrier recently secured a $500 million debt financing commitment backed by up to 22 aircraft, with an option to raise an additional $250 million using more planes as collateral.

The New York-based carrier reported a net loss of $319 million, or 86 cents per share, compared with $208 million, or 59 cents per share, reported a year earlier.

data.title: reAlpha Tech 10-Q: Revenue $0.84M, EPS $(0.03)
data.description:
reAlpha Tech reported quarterly revenue of $0.841 million and a net loss of $4.338 million, or $(0.03) per share, reflecting higher operating expenses that outpaced modest revenue. Revenue contracted from $0.926 million a year earlier while gross margin improved to 66% due to lower cost of revenues. The quarter included other expense and fair value adjustments contributing to the reported net loss.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0.841 million (down from $0.926 million prior year)
  • Revenue mix: Homebuying $0.577 million; Technology $0.264 million
  • Gross Profit: $0.552 million; Gross margin 66% (improved from 56%)
  • Operating Loss: $(4.281) million; Operating expenses increased to $4.833 million
  • Net Loss: $(4.338) million (includes $0.058 million other expense and fair value adjustments)
  • Net Loss per Share: $(0.03) basic and diluted; Weighted-average shares outstanding: 132.385 million

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix & growth: Overall revenue modestly declined year-over-year to $841K; technology services (AiChat) showed growth while homebuying revenue was weaker.
  • Channel & service shift: Transition from asset-heavy rentals toward a platform-focused model combining homebuying and technology services.
  • AI & product momentum: Launched and expanded AI agents including Claire, a Loan Officer Assistant, and an Engagement Agent to streamline customer workflows.
  • Geographic & service expansion: Homebuying services now operate in 35 states plus D.C., with full integration in Florida and Virginia and plans for nationwide rollout.
  • M&A & integration: Completed integration of Prevu and reAlpha Mortgage and proposed the InstaMortgage merger to add direct lending capabilities.

Original SEC Filing: reAlpha Tech Corp. [ AIRE ] - 10-Q - Apr. 28, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Shutterstock Q1: Revenue $199.2M, EPS $(1.34) on wider loss
data.description:
Shutterstock reported first-quarter revenue of $199.2 million and a diluted loss per share of $1.34, reflecting weaker new customer acquisition and timing in data licensing that pressured top-line performance. The company moved to an operating loss and a net loss as cost mix shifted higher, driven in part by AI token and hosting expenses. These results accompany ongoing strategic actions around product portfolios and the recently approved merger with Getty Images.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $199.2 million, down 18% year‑over‑year (constant currency -19%).
  • Gross Profit: $104.4 million (as reported in summaries).
  • Operating Income: $(30.954) million, compared with $10.201 million in the prior year (operating margin -15.6% vs 4.2%).
  • Net (loss) / income: $(47.569) million, compared with $18.688 million in the prior year.
  • (Losses) / Earnings per share - Diluted: $(1.34), prior year $0.53.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix and trends: Content remains the core of the business (88% of revenue) but content revenue declined 12% year‑over‑year. Data, Distribution & Services experienced a steeper drop (down 47%), attributed largely to timing of data licensing.
  • Customer acquisition and demand: The revenue decline was driven by weaker new customer acquisition and lower timing of data license deals.
  • Cost drivers and operations: Cost of revenue increased as a percentage of sales (48% versus 42% prior), reflecting higher AI token and hosting fees; marketing and product spend were optimized amid lower acquisition activity.
  • Portfolio and strategic positioning: Shutterstock’s brand and product portfolio includes Shutterstock, Pond5, TurboSquid, PicMonkey, PremiumBeat, Splash, Bigstock and Envato, with Studios and metadata offerings noted as growing strategic priorities.
  • M&A and regulatory developments: Shareholders approved the Getty Images merger; the U.K. Competition and Markets Authority’s Phase 2 review identified potential editorial remedies, influencing editorial divestiture planning.

Original SEC Filing: Shutterstock, Inc. [ SSTK ] - 10-Q - Apr. 28, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: JetBlue 10-Q: Revenue $2,240M, $0.86 Loss Per Share
data.description:
JetBlue reported total operating revenues of $2,240 million for the quarter, up $100 million (4.7%) year-over-year driven by higher passenger yield and ancillary revenue, while delivering a net loss of $319 million, or $0.86 per share. The company recorded an operating loss of $224 million (operating margin -10.0%), pressured by higher fuel costs and disruption-related expenses. Total operating expenses rose to $2,464 million, with CASM at 16.06 cents and CASM ex-fuel at 12.21 cents.

Financial Highlights

  • Total operating revenues: $2,240 million, up $100 million (4.7%) YoY
  • Total operating expenses: $2,464 million, up $150 million (6.5%)
  • Operating loss (Operating Income): $(224) million; operating margin (10.0)%
  • Net loss (Net Income): $(319) million, increased $111 million YoY
  • Loss per share (Net Income Per Share): $0.86 loss per share (basic and diluted); adjusted loss per share $0.87 excluding gains on investments
  • CASM: 16.06 cents; CASM ex-fuel: 12.21 cents

Business Highlights

  • Revenue momentum: Passenger revenue rose 4.0%; total operating revenue growth driven by higher yields and pricing.
  • Channel & product: Blue Sky interline with United launched; reciprocal loyalty benefits and Paisly non-air sales planned in second half of 2026.
  • Network & capacity: Fort Lauderdale capacity increased ~23% YoY with new year-round routes and expanded connectivity across Florida.
  • Operational performance: On-time performance fell to 68.8% and completion factor dropped to 95.0% amid severe weather and engine groundings; recovery initiatives are underway.
  • Fleet and maintenance: Continued retirement and sale of E190 aircraft; four aircraft temporarily grounded for engine inspections; committed A220 and A321 deliveries remain ongoing.

Original SEC Filing: JETBLUE AIRWAYS CORP [ JBLU ] - 10-Q - Apr. 28, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Armstrong World Industries 10-Q: Revenue $409.9M, EPS $1.55
data.description:
Armstrong World Industries reported quarterly net sales of $409.9 million and diluted earnings per share of $1.55, with revenue up 7.1% year-over-year driven by higher volumes and favorable average unit values. Gross profit was $155.3 million (37.9% margin) as higher raw material and energy costs partially offset productivity gains. Operating income declined to $94.2 million and net earnings were $66.8 million, with an effective tax rate of 24.4%.

Financial Highlights

  • Net sales: $409.9 million, up 7.1% vs prior year (volumes +$17M; favorable AUV +$10M)
  • Gross profit: $155.3 million; gross margin 37.9% (cost of goods sold 62.1% of sales)
  • Operating income: $94.2 million, down from $98.5 million year-over-year
  • Net earnings: $66.8 million, slightly below prior year ($69.1 million)
  • Net income per share: Diluted $1.55 per share (prior year $1.58); Basic $1.56 (prior year $1.59)

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth drivers: Consolidated net sales rose 7.1% year-over-year from higher volumes (+$17M) and favorable realized average unit value (+$10M).
  • Segment performance: Architectural Specialties grew 11%, reflecting $10 million of organic gains and $5 million from recent acquisitions.
  • Product and pricing momentum: Price increases in Mineral Fiber and WAVE grid products improved realized AUV and product mix.
  • Acquisitions and footprint: Completed Eventscape (Feb 2026), Parallel (Dec 2025), and Geometrik (Sep 2025) acquisitions; company now operates 24 plants.
  • Operational costs and productivity: Higher raw material and energy costs and a $2 million tariff pressured margins but were partially offset by productivity improvements.

Original SEC Filing: ARMSTRONG WORLD INDUSTRIES INC [ AWI ] - 10-Q - Apr. 28, 2026

Disclaimer

Overview

  • Communications real estate REIT's Q1 revenue rose 6.8%, beating analyst expectations

  • Adjusted EBITDA for Q1 beat consensus, up 5.2% year-over-year

  • Company repurchased $184 mln of shares during Q1

Outlook

  • American Tower raises 2026 property revenue outlook midpoint to $10.66 bln from prior view

  • Company now sees 2026 Adjusted EBITDA of $7.23 bln at midpoint, up from previous forecast

  • American Tower expects 2026 AFFO per share of $10.99 at midpoint, reflecting higher guidance

  • FOREIGN CURRENCY GAINS - Net income growth rates were significantly impacted by foreign currency gains in Q1 2026 versus losses in the prior-year period

data.title: ZAWYA: Axxion launches in Dubai as an independent motor claims management company for UAE insurers
data.description:
Dubai, United Arab Emirates, Axxion Claims Settlement Services L.L.C. has launched as an independent motor claims management company serving insurance partners in the United Arab Emirates. The Dubai-based company manages the full motor claims lifecycle on behalf of insurers, from first notification of loss through inspection, estimate review, repair coordination, salvage and total loss handling, third-party recovery, and closure. The service is built around a proprietary claims operating system designed to lower loss ratios, shorten claim cycle times, raise policyholder satisfaction, and recover costs from at-fault parties under audit-grade governance.

Axxion was founded to address known structural problems in the UAE motor claims market: long cycle times, inconsistent repair pricing, fragmented workshop standards, and claims leakage that drives up insurer expense ratios. The company gives insurers a single operating partner that takes accountability for the full lifecycle, run by experienced claims professionals, supported by automation, and measured against defined service commitments.

"Motor claims has been under-managed for years in the UAE," said Frederik Bisbjerg, Managing Director and Co-founder of Axxion. "Insurers know cycle times are too long and repair costs are inconsistent, but the operational tools to fix it have not been available at the right scale. Axxion exists to close that gap by running the operation, not just advising on it."

Behind the platform is a senior claims team. Axxion's operators have spent careers running motor claims at scale, drawn from regional and international insurers across underwriting, claims handling, repair management, and recovery. The operating model is built on the principle of people first, AI second: senior claims people make the judgment calls, and technology absorbs the repetitive load. Insurer partners deal with named operators, not a support queue. Since launch, a handful of UAE motor insurers have signed up for Axxion's services, with further pilot conversations under way across the market. Early operating results on managed portfolios point to material reductions in claim cost and cycle time versus insurer baselines, alongside measurable improvements in policyholder satisfaction.

"The technology is in service of the operation, not the other way around," said Stijn Venrooij, Executive Director of Operations and AI at Axxion. "AI helps the team move faster on routine work and concentrates human expertise where it matters most. Insurers see the result as a better claim experience for their policyholders and a more predictable cost line on their P&L."

Axxion is part of the Skelmore Group, a diversified automotive and insurance services group with operations across the wider MENA region, the United States, and Canada. The group employs around 4,000 people and reports approximately AED 2.4 billion in annual revenue under consolidated financials. Within the Middle East, Axxion sits inside World Automotive Group (WAG), which has been continuously active in the regional automotive and insurance services market for over three decades. The institutional backing means Axxion enters the market with the financial depth and operational stability required for long-term insurer partnerships.

Near-term plans include the rollout of additional insurer engagements, continued expansion of the workshop network across the Emirates, and evaluation of further GCC market entry.

About Axxion

Axxion Claims Settlement Services L.L.C. is an independent motor claims management company based in Dubai. The company provides end-to-end motor claims handling for insurance partners across the United Arab Emirates, built around a proprietary claims operating system that combines experienced claims operators, a governed workshop network, and AI-enabled workflows. Axxion is part of the Skelmore Group, a diversified automotive and insurance services group operating across the wider MENA region, the United States, and Canada, with around 4,000 employees and approximately AED 2.4 billion in annual revenue. In the Middle East, Axxion sits within Skelmore's regional operating company, World Automotive Group (WAG). More information is available at axxion.co.

*Source: AETOSWire

Contacts

Frederik Bisbjerg

Managing Director & Co-Founder

Axxion Claims Settlement Services L.L.C. ‪

f@axxion.c

data.title: Vestis Announces Date for Fiscal Second Quarter 2026 Results Conference Call and Webcast
data.description:
ATLANTA--(BUSINESS WIRE)--April 28, 2026--

Vestis , a leading provider of uniforms and workplace supplies, today announced that it will issue its fiscal second quarter 2026 results before the market opens on Tuesday, May 12, 2026. A conference call will be held that same morning at 8:30 a.m. ET to review the Company's financial results and conduct a question-and-answer session.

A webcast of the conference call and accompanying presentation materials will be available in the Investor Relations section of the Company's website at https://ir.vestis.com. To listen to a live broadcast, go to the site at least 15 minutes prior to the scheduled start time in order to register, download, and install any necessary audio software.

To participate in the live teleconference:

United States Live: 800-267-6316

International Live: 203-518-9783

Access Code: VSTSQ226

A replay of the live event will also be available on the Company's website shortly after the conclusion of the call.

About VESTIS(TM)

Vestis is a leader in the B2B uniform and workplace supplies category. Vestis provides uniform services and workplace supplies to a broad range of North American customers from Fortune 500 companies to locally owned small businesses across a broad set of end sectors. The Company's comprehensive service offering primarily includes a full-service uniform rental program, floor mats, towels, linens, managed restroom services, first aid supplies, and cleanroom and other specialty garment processing.

data.title: UPS Earnings Beat Estimates. Why the Stock Is Falling Anyway. — Barrons.com
data.description:
Al Root

United Parcel Service reported better-than-expected first-quarter numbers. Investors still look nervous. They don't like waiting for improvement in quarters to come. They want to see it now.

Tuesday morning, the shipping company reported first-quarter earnings per share of $1.07 from sales of $21.2 billion. Wall Street was looking for earnings per share of $1.01 and operating profit of $1.3 billion from sales of $21 billion, according to FactSet.

A year ago, UPS reported earnings per share of $1.49 and operating profit of $1.8 billion from sales of $21.5 billion. Adjusted operating profit margins in the quarter were 6.2%, in line with Wall Street estimates, but down from 8.2% a year ago.

The company maintained its full-year outlook. In January, UPS projected 2026 sales of $89.7 billion and operating profit of $8.6 billion. Wall Street currently projects sales of about $89.6 billion and operating profit of $8.5 billion.

Capital expenditures are expected to be about $3 billion, and dividend payments are expected to be about $5.4 billion, indicating no change in the quarterly payout.

Shares were down 2.9% at $105.06 in premarket trading, while S&P 500 futures were down 0.3% and Dow Jones Industrial Average futures were up 0.3%.

Volume in the company's U.S. domestic segment declined, as expected. The company is leaving some low-profit Amazon.com business. There are a couple of more quarters left of weak year-over-year Amazon comparisons.

"The first quarter of 2026 marked a critical transition period for UPS in which we needed to flawlessly execute several major strategic actions, and we delivered," said CEO Carol Tome in a news release. "With that behind us, we expect to return to consolidated revenue and operating profit growth, and adjusted operating margin expansion in the second quarter of this year."

The quarter looks fine, with numbers in line or slightly better than expected, but investors might have wanted to see more improvement now rather than wait for it in future quarters.

Coming into Tuesday trading, UPS stock was up 9% this year and up 11% over the past 12 months.

That still leaves shares down about 47% over the past five years. UPS has struggled through a freight recession in an inflationary environment.

In 2021, UPS generated an operating profit margin of 13.5% from sales of $97.3 billion, according to FactSet. In 2025, the margin was 9.8% from $88.7 billion in sales.

Leaving the Amazon business behind has also weighed on sales growth.

Declines have left shares trading for less than 15 times earnings projected over the coming 12 months. Five years ago, the stock traded for about 18 times earnings.

Write to Al Root at allen.root@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Ares Capital Q1 adjusted EPS misses analyst estimates
data.description:
Overview

  • US specialty finance firm's Q1 adjusted EPS missed analyst expectations

  • Net investment income rose yr/yr, while GAAP net income per share declined

  • Company maintained quarterly dividend at $0.48 per share

Outlook

  • Ares Capital declares Q2 2026 dividend of $0.48 per share

  • Company says improving lending conditions with enhanced spreads and more attractive terms

  • Ares Capital cites strong liquidity and stable dividend as reflecting earnings power in current market

Result Drivers

  • PORTFOLIO PERFORMANCE - Co cited healthy portfolio performance and low levels of non-accruing investments as supporting results, per CEO Kort Schnabel

  • LENDING CONDITIONS - Management said improving lending conditions, including enhanced spreads and fees and more attractive terms, contributed to results

  • CAPITAL STRUCTURE - Co raised over $1.25 bln of new debt financing at attractive terms, enhancing liquidity and flexibility, per CFO Scott Lem

data.title: Controladora Vuela Compania de Aviacion SAB de CV reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Controladora Vuela Compania de Aviacion SAB de CV reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 4 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, lower than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of -45 cents. The mean expectation of eight analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 23 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 6 cents tozero cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 30.1% to $770.00 million from a year ago; analysts expected $890.97 million.

  • Controladora Vuela Compania de Aviacion SAB de CV's reported EPS for the quarter was 4 cents​.

  • The company reported a quarterly loss of $71 million.

  • Controladora Vuela Compania de Aviacion SAB de CV shares had risen by 1.1% this quarter and lost 17.6% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 105.9% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 7 "strong buy" or "buy," 5 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the airlines peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Controladora Vuela Compania de Aviacion SAB de CV is $8.50, about 13.9% above its last closing price of $7.32

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data April 28 at 11:08 a.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: BankFirst Q1 net income rises, net interest income up 30%
data.description:
Overview

  • U.S. regional bank's Q1 net income rose yr/yr, supported by loan and deposit growth

  • Net interest income for Q1 grew 30% yr/yr as cost of funds declined

  • Company repurchased 50,000 shares under previously announced buyback program

Outlook

  • BankFirst expects to maintain eligibility for 1.25% dividend rate on Senior Preferred for next annual cycle

  • Company says economic uncertainty and elevated rates may require additional credit loss provisions

Result Drivers

  • LOAN AND DEPOSIT GROWTH - Co said steady organic loan and deposit growth supported Q1 results

  • DECLINING COST OF FUNDS - Co said a lower cost of funds contributed to net interest margin expansion

  • MAGNOLIA ACQUISITION IMPACT - Completion of the Magnolia Acquisition in July 2025 contributed to increases in total assets and deposits yr/yr

data.title: Asbury Automotive Q1 revenue misses estimates
data.description:
Overview

  • US auto retailer's Q1 revenue fell 1% and missed analyst expectations

  • Adjusted EPS for Q1 missed analyst expectations

  • Company repurchased $147 mln in shares and divested ten dealerships for $210 mln

Outlook

  • Company expects efficiency gains and improved performance as year progresses due to foundational investments

Result Drivers

  • DEALERSHIP DIVESTITURES - Co divested ten dealerships and terminated seven franchises, reducing annualized revenue by an estimated $625 mln

  • HIGHER SG&A EXPENSES - SG&A as a percentage of gross profit rose to 70.2% from 63.0% a year earlier, pressuring margins

data.title: Air Canada expected to post a loss of 42 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Air Canada is expected to show a rise in quarterly revenue when it reports results on April 30 for the period ending March 31 2026

  • The Saint-laurent Quebec-based company is expected to report a 3.2% increase in revenue to C$5.363 billion from C$5.2 billion a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 13 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Air Canada is for a loss of 42 cents per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 8 "strong buy" or "buy," 7 "hold" and 1 "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 0.2% in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Air Canada is C$22.00, about 18.8% above its last closing price of C$18.52
